articles.title: Tamil Nadu, Kerala news LIVE updates: AIADMK's warning to MPs ahead of Vijay trust vote; Kerala CM announcement today

After several deliberations and delay, Congress is expected to announce its CM pick today.

articles.title: Oil prices slip on teetering Iran ceasefire as Trump heads to China
articles.description: Oil prices dipped as investors awaited developments on the Iran ceasefire and President Trump's summit with China's Xi Jinping. Brent crude futures fell to $106.95 a barrel, and WTI futures to $101.52. The ongoing conflict and supply disruptions are expected to keep oil prices above $80 for the year.

articles.title: Henry Wang on US–China Summit Expectations
articles.description:
Henry Huiyao Wang, Founder and President of the Center for China and Globalization, says both Iran and the United States are seeking to de-escalate the ongoing conflict. 
He adds that China could provide a platform for both sides to “gracefully climb down the ladder” if U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping reach a consensus at the upcoming US–China summit.
Wang speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts in Beijing on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks track Wall Street lower on US CPI
articles.description: Asian stocks declined as US inflation accelerated, influenced by rising oil prices following the conflict in Iran. This prompted an increase in Treasury yields and boosted bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2027. Elevated oil prices and inflation risks now threaten the recent equity rebound, particularly impacting chipmakers.

He highlighted India's stance against terrorism, particularly from foreign soil, and underscored the Indian Navy's role as the guardian of the increasingly contested Indo-Pacific maritime domain.

articles.title: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of the worst" in history
articles.description: Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey is pushing back primaries for four Congressional districts following Monday's Supreme Court decision allowing the state to use a 2023 map with only one majority-Black district. Other states are also looking to redraw their maps after the decision. NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

articles.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.

articles.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.

articles.title: Rahul 'strongly dissents' on next CBI director's selection; talks concluded in just 5 minutes
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi registered a strong dissent against the CBI director selection process, calling it a "biased exercise" and a "mere formality." He alleged the government denied him crucial information, including candidate appraisal reports, preventing a meaningful role. Gandhi stated he would not be a "rubber stamp" and boycotted the meeting.

articles.title: Sebi may relax norms for select agri commodity F&O contracts
articles.description: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has proposed a pilot program to allow select agricultural commodity derivatives to trade as cash-settled instruments before mandatory physical settlement. This initiative aims to boost liquidity and market confidence in agricultural contracts, with commodities like maize, groundnut, and chilli being considered for the pilot.

articles.title: WATCH:  18-wheeler slams into vehicles waiting at traffic light in North Carolina

articles.title: IT stocks tumble on OpenAI's push into AI adoption

articles.title: Kerala CM suspense may end today, Congress wraps up final huddle

Senior leaders, including Rahul and Sonia Gandhi, are expected to finalize the successor to the Left Front's decade-long rule. The prolonged uncertainty has somewhat dulled the UDF's election victory, with three prominent figures vying for the top post.

articles.title: Govt's new onion strategy focuses on local procurement, storage
articles.description: The Centre is working on a decentralized onion procurement and storage plan to help states build local buffer stocks, reduce wastage and curb sharp price spikes. The move aims to stabilize supplies, cool food inflation and improve farmer incomes.

articles.title: Sam Altman was winning on the stand, but it might not be enough
articles.description:
Who, me? | Image: The Verge; Getty Images	

After two weeks of hearing from assorted witnesses that he was a lying snake, the jury finally heard from the lying snake himself: Sam Altman. At the end of the testimony, his lawyer William Savitt asked him how it felt to be accused of stealing a charity.
"We created, through a ton of hard work, this extremely large charity, and I agree you can't steal it," Altman said. "Mr. Musk did try to kill it, I guess. Twice." 
Altman was fully in "nice kid from St. Louis" mode, and did a passable impression of a man who was bewildered at what was happening to him. When he stepped down from the stand holding a stack of evidence binders, he even look …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: iOS 27 might add a lot more customization to the Camera app
articles.description:
Apple's next iOS update could include something phone photographers have been waiting for: a lot more control over the Camera app. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the Camera app will be "fully customizable" in iOS 27 and users will be able to "pick their own set of controls - called widgets - that run along the top of the interface."
Gurman notes that the Camera app will keep the current set of default widgets, but starting with iOS 27, you will "be able to switch over to an 'advanced' array of options or choose their own items." From there, you'll reportedly be able to select from a new "Add Widgets" tray, with widgets split into thr …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
articles.description:
Meta announced on Tuesday that it's testing a Threads feature that lets users tag a Meta AI account to get answers to questions or context about a conversation on the platform. If you've spent any time looking at replies on X as of late, this new feature sounds a lot like Meta's take on people tagging xAI's Grok. But, as reported by Engadget, Threads users quickly discovered that you can't block the new Meta AI account, and they aren't happy about it.
Meta has invested heavily in AI as it works to catch up to rivals like OpenAI and Google, spending billions to hire AI talent. It launched a new AI model called Muse Spark in April, which it s …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Meta will tell parents when their teens add new interests to their Instagram algorithm
articles.description:
Starting Tuesday, parents can get more details about what their teens are up to on Instagram. Meta is adding a feature to its Teen Accounts that shows parents the "general topics their teens engage with," like "basketball" or "fashion." Meta also says it will soon start notifying parents when their teen adds a new interest to their algorithm.
In December, Meta announced its "Your Algorithm" feature, which lets users choose topics they want to see more or less of in Instagram. Meta noted in its Tuesday blog post that users with Teen Accounts are already limited in what topics they can add to their algorithm - content shown to users with teen …

articles.title: Diplomacy and sustainability- dialogues for the future
articles.description: The Hindu Group, in association with Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences (SIMATS), will host The Hindu Diplomacy & Sustainability Dialogues 2026 on May 13 at SIMATS, Chennai

articles.title: Tamil Nadu, Kerala news LIVE updates: AIADMK's warning to MPs ahead of Vijay trust vote; Kerala CM announcement today
articles.description: Tamil Nadu, Kerala news LIVE updates: Congress came back to power in Kerala after a decade of the Left rule, as its coalition United Democratic Front won a total of 102 seats out of 104 in the state assembly. After several deliberations and delay, Congress is expected to announce its CM pick today.

articles.title: Will announce pick for LoP after Cong announces CM: LDF
articles.description: Kerala's LDF will announce its Leader of Opposition after Congress names its chief minister. Contenders include Pinarayi Vijayan and KN Balagopal.

articles.title: Cruise passenger shows life inside Nebraska quarantine after hantavirus exposure scare
articles.description: MV Hondius cruise ship passenger documents his quarantine experience at a Nebraska medical facility as officials monitor a hantavirus outbreak on board.

articles.title: House GOP launches new task force, probes alleged $250B Medicaid fraud in Ohio
articles.description: House Republicans launched a new Oversight task force, beginning with an investigation into alleged Medicaid fraud by Ohio home health companies.

articles.title: Anxious aspirants call NEET UG paper leak ‘demotivating’, say ‘years of exam prep’ toppled
articles.description: Abhishek Verma, an aspirant from Lucknow, called the news ‘demotivating’, saying restarting the final phase of preparation would be mentally draining.

articles.title: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill announces another NJ Transit World Cup ticket cut days after first reduction
articles.description: NJ Transit 2026 FIFA World Cup train ticket prices cut again to $98 round-trip after Gov. Mikie Sherrill revealed sponsors that helped lower costs for fans.

articles.title: For Chinese exporters, Iran worries eclipse tariff woes as Trump, Xi prepare to meet
articles.description: Heading into the summit, Beijing and Washington will likely reaffirm their shared intention to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore stability in the region.

topStories.description: MV Hondius cruise ship passenger documents his quarantine experience at a Nebraska medical facility as officials monitor a hantavirus outbreak on board.

topStories.description: House Republicans launched a new Oversight task force, beginning with an investigation into alleged Medicaid fraud by Ohio home health companies.

topStories.description: NJ Transit 2026 FIFA World Cup train ticket prices cut again to $98 round-trip after Gov. Mikie Sherrill revealed sponsors that helped lower costs for fans.

topStories.title: For Chinese exporters, Iran worries eclipse tariff woes as Trump, Xi prepare to meet
topStories.description: Heading into the summit, Beijing and Washington will likely reaffirm their shared intention to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore stability in the region.

topStories.title: Former private prison official to serve as acting ICE chief
topStories.description: Compared to the start of President Trump's second term, David Venturella takes over an agency with a larger workforce, more financial resources — and also a continued funding lapse.

topStories.title: Oil prices slip on teetering Iran ceasefire as Trump heads to China
topStories.description: Oil prices dipped as investors awaited developments on the Iran ceasefire and President Trump's summit with China's Xi Jinping. Brent crude futures fell to $106.95 a barrel, and WTI futures to $101.52. The ongoing conflict and supply disruptions are expected to keep oil prices above $80 for the year.

topStories.title: Republican Ricketts wins GOP Primary in Nebraska, advances to key Senate showdown
topStories.description: Republican Sen. Pete Ricketts wins Nebraska's GOP Senate primary, setting up an expected competitive general election against independent Dan Osborn.

topStories.title: Henry Wang on US–China Summit Expectations
topStories.description:
Henry Huiyao Wang, Founder and President of the Center for China and Globalization, says both Iran and the United States are seeking to de-escalate the ongoing conflict. 
He adds that China could provide a platform for both sides to “gracefully climb down the ladder” if U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping reach a consensus at the upcoming US–China summit.
Wang speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts in Beijing on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks track Wall Street lower on US CPI
topStories.description: Asian stocks declined as US inflation accelerated, influenced by rising oil prices following the conflict in Iran. This prompted an increase in Treasury yields and boosted bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2027. Elevated oil prices and inflation risks now threaten the recent equity rebound, particularly impacting chipmakers.

topStories.title: Democrats pick Nebraska Senate nominee who could drop out and back independent Dan Osborn
topStories.description: Cindy Burbank won the Democratic Senate primary in Nebraska, NBC News projects, potentially clearing the way for independent Dan Osborn to take on GOP Sen.

topStories.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
topStories.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.

topStories.title: Big city Democrat mayor arrested at ICE protest faces voters in re-election bid
topStories.description: Ras Baraka, the Newark mayor arrested at an anti-ICE protest, won re-election Tuesday with over 50% of the vote, avoiding a November runoff election.

dailyNews.title: Justice Dept. Officials Consider Settling Trump Suit Against I.R.S.
dailyNews.description: One of the settlement terms under review is for the I.R.S. to drop any audits of the president, his family members and businesses.

dailyNews.title: New York Times defends controversial anti-Israel piece, 'no truth' to retraction claims
dailyNews.description: The New York Times defended columnist Nicholas Kristof's controversial piece alleging Palestinian sexual abuse amid retraction rumors and Israeli backlash.

dailyNews.title: What to know about today's primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
dailyNews.description: Voters are going to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

dailyNews.title: Will Do Whatever Necessary': Trump Signals Flexibility On Russian Oil Waiver
dailyNews.description: Speaking about peace deal with Iran, Trump said that the US is only making a good deal.

dailyNews.title: 5/12: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: President Trump heads to Bejing to meet with Xi Jinping; inflation surges to its highest level in nearly three years.

dailyNews.title: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of the worst" in history
dailyNews.description: Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey is pushing back primaries for four Congressional districts following Monday's Supreme Court decision allowing the state to use a 2023 map with only one majority-Black district. Other states are also looking to redraw their maps after the decision. NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Why health officials expect hantavirus cases to continue rising
dailyNews.description: Global health officials are warning that hantavirus cases stemming from a cruise ship outbreak could continue to rise. Dr. Abraar Karan, with Stanford University's division of infectious diseases, joins CBS News to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Hantavirus outbreak's unknown origins addressed by medical experts
dailyNews.description: As epidemiologists race to find more answers to the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, they have addressed some theories about its origins.

dailyNews.title: Iran war, trade, tariffs – What Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are set to discuss in China
dailyNews.description: For the next two days, Trump will participate in bilateral meetings with Xi Jinping. A state banquet and a working lunch are also planned during Trump's visit.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  18-wheeler slams into vehicles waiting at traffic light in North Carolina
dailyNews.description: Video captured the harrowing moment an 18-wheeler plowed into vehicles waiting at a traffic light in Goldsboro, North Carolina. At least nine people were injured, including two who were airlifted.

dailyNews.title: Iran war live: Trump travels to China as conflict with Tehran looms large
dailyNews.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed 2,883 people and injured 8,787 since March 2, Lebanon's Health Ministry says.

dailyNews.title: Hantavirus live updates: Patient in France presenting 'severe form' of virus
dailyNews.description: The total number of confirmed and probable cases of hantavirus onboard the MV Hondius cruise ship has risen to 11.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Expected to Tap Longtime ICE Official to Lead Agency
dailyNews.description: David Venturella’s expected appointment is in line with a push by Markwayne Mullin, the homeland security secretary, to keep his department under the radar and out of headlines.

dailyNews.title: Trump-Xi summit in Beijing: What's at stake
dailyNews.description: Agenda for talks between Presidents Trump and Xi Jinping highlights the array of U.S. interests that depend on — and often clash with — Beijing's policies.

dailyNews.title: US Taiwan policy will be watched closely during Trump's China visit
dailyNews.description: While the conflict in Iran will loom over Trump's China summit, what might be the most consequential issue discussed behind closed doors is much closer to Beijing.

dailyNews.title: ‘I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation’: Trump says stopping Iran nuclear program is ‘all’ that matters
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday said that he is not concerned regarding the economic impact of the US-Iran war on Americans, adding that the only thing that matters is that Tehran cannot have a nuclear weapon.

dailyNews.title: Some Republicans aren’t sold on $1B price tag for Trump’s ballroom project
dailyNews.description: Senate Republicans got a Secret Service briefing on how the proposed taxpayer funds would be spent. Several said they still aren’t convinced.

dailyNews.title: FDA chief resigns after Trump admin forced approval of fruity e-cigs
dailyNews.description: Makary reportedly spent his year bucking Trump admin and making industry enemies.

dailyNews.title: How Americans see China now
dailyNews.description: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows Iran Retains Substantial Missile Capabilities
dailyNews.description: Secret new assessments say Iran has operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that its military remains far stronger than President Trump has asserted.

dailyNews.title: Why the Iran war is hurting the economy
dailyNews.description: There isn’t much Congress or Trump can do about it, experts warn

dailyNews.title: MAHA Keeps Being Weird as Hell About Fertility
dailyNews.description: RFK Jr. and Dr. Mehmet Oz’s comments about teen sperm count and “underbabied” Americans at a recent women’s health event underscore the White House’s pronatalist agenda.

dailyNews.title: Auto-state lawmakers seek to keep Chinese EV parts out of U.S. as Trump heads to Beijing
dailyNews.description: Michigan lawmakers introduced a bipartisan bill to ban Chinese-connected vehicles and technology from the U.S. market over security concerns.

dailyNews.title: Marty Makary resigns as FDA commissioner following industry and White House backlash
dailyNews.description: Makary's tenure was marked by internal dysfunction at the FDA, leadership turmoil and mounting backlash from drugmakers and physicians on regulatory decisions.
